name: Protect
effect: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

name: Fake Out
effect: This attack hits first and makes the target flinch. It works only on the first turn each time the user enters battle.

name: Tailwind
effect: The user whips up a turbulent whirlwind that boosts the Speed stats of itself and its allies for four turns.

name: Rock Slide
effect: Large boulders are hurled at opposing Pokémon to inflict damage. This may also make the opposing Pokémon flinch.

name: Moonblast
effect: Borrowing the power of the moon, the user attacks the target. Has a 10% chance of lowering the target's Sp. Atk stat by 1 stage.

name: Weather Ball
effect: This move's type and power change depending on the weather at the time the move is used.

name: Close Combat
effect: The user fights the target up close, inflicting damage without guarding itself. This also lowers the user's Defense and Sp. Def stats.

name: Parting Shot
effect: With a parting threat, the user lowers the target's Attack and Sp. Atk stats. Then it switches places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

name: Wave Crash
effect: The user shrouds itself in water and slams into the target with its whole body to inflict damage. This also damages the user quite a lot.

name: Earthquake
effect: The user sets off an earthquake that strikes every Pokémon around it.

name: Trick Room
effect: The user creates a bizarre area in which slower Pokémon get to move first for five turns.

name: Heat Wave
effect: The user attacks by exhaling hot breath on opposing Pokémon. This may also leave them with a burn.

name: Iron Head
effect: The user slams the target with its steel-hard head. Has a 20% chance of making the target flinch.

name: Dragon Claw
effect: The user slashes the target with huge, sharp claws to inflict damage.

name: Rage Powder
effect: The user scatters a cloud of irritating powder to draw attention to itself. Opposing Pokémon aim only at the user.

name: Flare Blitz
effect: The user cloaks itself in fire and charges the target to inflict damage. This also damages the user quite a lot and may leave the target with a burn.

name: Sucker Punch
effect: This move enables the user to attack first. This move fails if the target is not readying an attack.

name: Aqua Jet
effect: The user lunges at the target to inflict damage, moving at blinding speed. This move always goes first.

name: Last Respects
effect: The user attacks to avenge its allies. The more defeated allies there are in the user's party, the greater the move's power.

name: Matcha Gotcha
effect: The user fires a blast of tea that it mixed. The user's HP is restored by up to half the damage taken by the target. This may also leave the target with a burn.

name: Encore
effect: The user compels the target to keep using the move it encored for three turns.

name: Kowtow Cleave
effect: The user slashes at the target after kowtowing to make the target let down its guard. This attack never misses.

name: Shadow Ball
effect: The user attacks by hurling a shadowy blob at the target. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

name: Solar Beam
effect: The user gathers light on the first turn, then blasts a bundled beam on the next turn.

name: Dazzling Gleam
effect: The user damages opposing Pokémon by emitting a powerful flash.

name: Hurricane
effect: The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind. This may also confuse the target.

name: Light Screen
effect: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from special moves for five turns.

name: Dragon Pulse
effect: The target is attacked with a shock wave generated by the user's gaping mouth.

name: Hyper Voice
effect: The user attacks by letting loose a horribly loud, resounding cry.

name: Reflect
effect: A wondrous wall of light is put up to reduce damage from physical moves for five turns.

name: Electro Shot
effect: The user gathers electricity on the first turn, boosting its Sp. Atk stat, then fires a high-voltage shot on the next turn. The shot will be fired immediately in rain.

name: Flash Cannon
effect: The user gathers all its light energy and releases it at once. This may also lower the target's Sp. Def stat.

name: Helping Hand
effect: The user assists an ally by boosting the power of that ally's attack.

name: Ice Punch
effect: The target is attacked with an icy punch. This may also leave the target frozen.

name: Life Dew
effect: The user scatters mysterious water around and restores the HP of itself and its ally Pokémon currently in the battle.

name: Stomping Tantrum
effect: Driven by frustration, the user attacks the target. This move's power is doubled if the user's previous move failed.

name: Throat Chop
effect: The user attacks the target's throat, and the resultant suffering prevents the target from using sound-based moves for two turns.

name: Wide Guard
effect: The user and its allies are protected from wide-ranging attacks for one turn.

name: Brave Bird
effect: The user tucks in its wings and charges at a low altitude. This also damages the user quite a lot.

name: Dire Claw
effect: The user lashes out at the target with ruinous claws. Has a 30% chance of leaving the target poisoned paralyzed or asleep.

name: Earth Power
effect: The user makes the ground under the target erupt with power. This may also lower the target's Sp. Def stat.

name: Psychic
effect: The target is hit with a strong telekinetic force to inflict damage. This may also lower the target’s Sp. Def stat.

name: Psychic Fangs
effect: The user attacks by biting the target with its psychic capabilities. This move can also break barriers, such as Light Screen and Reflect.

name: Thunderbolt
effect: The user attacks the target with a strong electric blast. This may also leave the target with paralysis.

name: Dual Wingbeat
effect: The user slams the target with its wings to inflict damage. The target is hit twice in a row.

name: Flip Turn
effect: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

name: Low Kick

name: Nasty Plot
effect: The user stimulates its brain by thinking bad thoughts. This sharply boosts the user's Sp. Atk stat.

name: Sludge Bomb
effect: The user hurls unsanitary sludge at the target to inflict damage. This may also poison the target.

name: Calm Mind
effect: The user quietly focuses its mind and calms its spirit to boost its Sp. Atk and Sp. Def stats.

name: Detect
effect: This move enables the user to protect itself from all attacks. Its chance of failing rises if it is used in succession.

name: Hyper Beam

name: Make It Rain

name: Quick Attack

name: Roost

name: Spirit Break

name: Will-O-Wisp
effect: The user shoots a sinister flame at the target to inflict a burn.

name: Blizzard

name: Darkest Lariat
effect: The user swings both arms and hits the target. The target's stat changes don't affect the damage inflicted by this move.

name: Focus Blast
effect: The user heightens its mental focus and unleashes its power. This may also lower the target's Sp. Def stat.

name: High Horsepower
effect: The user fiercely attacks the target using its entire body.

name: Knock Off

name: Light of Ruin

name: Overheat

name: Swords Dance

name: Aurora Veil

name: Body Press

name: Coaching

name: Dark Pulse

name: Eruption

name: Follow Me

name: Foul Play

name: Giga Drain

name: Poison Jab

name: Volt Switch
effect: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

name: Zap Cannon

name: Aura Sphere
effect: The user lets loose a pulse of aura power from deep within its body at the target. This attack never misses.

name: Bulk Up
effect: The user tenses its muscles to bulk up its body, boosting its Attack and Defense stats.

name: Bullet Punch

name: Charm

name: Energy Ball

name: Fake Tears

name: Hydro Pump

name: Ice Beam
effect: The target is struck with an icy-cold beam of energy. This may also leave the target frozen.

name: Ice Fang
effect: The user bites with cold-infused fangs. This may also make the target flinch or leave it frozen.

name: Icy Wind
effect: The user attacks with a gust of chilled air. This also lowers opposing Pokémon's Speed stats.

name: Liquidation

name: Rock Tomb

name: Taunt

name: Twin Beam
effect: The user shoots mystical beams from its eyes to inflict damage. The target is hit twice in a row.

name: U-turn
effect: After making its attack, the user rushes back to switch places with a party Pokémon in waiting.

name: Yawn
effect: The user lets loose a huge yawn that lulls the target into falling asleep on the next turn.

name: Accelerock
effect: The user smashes into the target at high speed. This move always goes first.

name: After You
effect: The user helps the target and makes it use its move right after the user.

name: Air Slash
effect: The user attacks with a blade of air that slices even the sky. This may also make the target flinch.

name: Crunch

name: Hypnosis
effect: The user employs hypnotic suggestion to make the target fall asleep.

name: Ice Shard
effect: The user flash-freezes chunks of ice and hurls them at the target. This move always goes first.

name: Icicle Crash
effect: The user attacks by crashing large icicles onto the target. This may also make the target flinch.

name: Imprison
effect: If opposing Pokémon know any move also known by the user, they are prevented from using it.

name: Infestation

name: Last Resort
effect: This move can be used only after the user has used all the other moves it knows in the battle.

name: Leaf Storm
effect: The user whips up a storm of leaves around the target. The recoil from this move harshly lowers the user's Sp. Atk stat.

name: Leech Seed

name: Meteor Mash
effect: The target is hit with a hard punch fired like a meteor. This may also boost the user’s Attack stat.

name: Muddy Water

name: Poltergeist

name: Psycho Cut
effect: The user tears at the target with blades formed by psychic power. This move has a heightened chance of landing a critical hit.

name: Quash
effect: The user suppresses the target and makes its move go last.

name: Quick Guard
effect: The user protects itself and its allies from priority moves.

name: Rage Fist
effect: The user converts its rage into energy to attack. The more times the user has been hit by attacks, the greater the move's power.

name: Recover
effect: The user regenerates its cells, restoring its own HP by up to half its max HP.

name: Roar

name: Sacred Sword
effect: The user attacks by slicing with a sword. The target's stat changes don't affect the damage inflicted by this move.

name: Scald

name: Scary Face
effect: The user frightens the target with a scary face to harshly lower its Speed stat.

name: Ancient Power

name: Apple Acid
